description:
As a developer using browser-node
  I want to run an Express.js server
  So that I can serve HTTP routes without a real backend

elements.name: Express server starts and announces its port

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Express server running"

elements.name: Express handles a named route
Syntax error in Express code surfaces in the terminal

arguments.content:
const express = require('express'
// missing closing paren — syntax error

steps.name: the terminal should show a runtime error

elements.name: Express middleware chain works

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Express middleware server running"

name: Express.js HTTP server

description:
As a developer using browser-node
  I want to run a Fastify server
  So that I can use a high-performance Node.js framework in the browser

elements.name: Fastify server starts successfully

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Fastify server running"

elements.name: Fastify handles async route handlers

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Fastify async route ready"

elements.name: Fastify reports plugin/decorator errors at boot

steps.name: the terminal should show a runtime error
the terminal should NOT contain "should not reach here"

elements.name: Fastify with JSON schema validation

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Fastify schema server running"

name: Fastify HTTP server

description:
As a developer using browser-node
  I want to use React with SSR
  So that I can render React components to HTML strings

elements.name: React renders a component to HTML string

steps.name: the terminal should contain "React SSR complete"

elements.name: React runs an HTTP server with SSR

steps.name: the terminal should contain "React server running"

elements.name: React createElement with props and children

steps.name: the terminal should contain "React props+children: ok"

elements.name: React missing module causes an error

arguments.content:
const React = require('react')
const Missing = require('nonexistent-react-addon')

steps.name: the terminal should show a runtime error

name: React server-side rendering

description:
As a developer using browser-node
  I want to load Angular core packages
  So that I can use Angular's reactive primitives and dependency injection utilities

elements.name: Angular core and RxJS BehaviorSubject work correctly

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Angular server running"

elements.name: Angular version is accessible

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Angular version check passed"

elements.name: RxJS Observable operators work

steps.name: the terminal should contain "RxJS operators working correctly"

elements.name: Angular core error propagates to terminal

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Angular failed: deliberate Angular test error"

name: Angular core and RxJS

description:
As a developer using browser-node
  I want to start a Next.js dev server
  So that I can build React applications with Next.js in the browser

elements.name: Next.js dev server starts and announces its URL

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Next.js server running"

elements.name: Next.js prepare step logs progress

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Next.js second instance running"

name: Next.js development server

description:
As a developer using browser-node
  I want to use Vue.js with SSR
  So that I can render Vue components to HTML strings

elements.name: Vue renders a component with createSSRApp

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Vue server running"

elements.name: Vue component with props renders correctly

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Vue props test passed"

elements.name: Vue reactive data updates propagate

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Vue reactivity working correctly"

elements.name: Vue missing module causes an error

arguments.content:
const { createSSRApp } = require('vue')
const missing = require('@vue/nonexistent-plugin')

steps.name: the terminal should show a runtime error
the terminal should NOT contain "Vue server running"

name: Vue.js server-side rendering

description:
As a developer using browser-node
  I want to run a Vite dev server
  So that I can serve and transform frontend projects from inside the browser

elements.name: Vite dev server starts and announces its URL

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Vite dev server running"

elements.name: Vite transforms a JavaScript module

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Vite JS transform works"

elements.name: Vite transforms a TypeScript file

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Vite TypeScript transform works"

elements.name: Vite build throws because bundler is not supported in browser

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Vite build correctly rejected"

elements.name: Two Vite servers run on separate ports simultaneously

steps.name: the terminal should contain "Both Vite servers running"

name: Vite dev server inside browser-node

elements.name: echo prints text to the terminal

steps.name: I run terminal command "echo hello from terminal"
the terminal should contain "hello from terminal"

elements.name: ls lists files after creating one
mkdir -p creates nested directory
touch creates an empty file
cat reads a file's contents

steps.name: I create file "/app/greet.txt" with content "hello cat"

elements.name: cd changes working directory
node executes a JS file and shows output

steps.name: I create file "/app/hello.js" with content "console.log('node works')"

elements.name: running node on the same file twice produces output both times

steps.name: I create file "/app/counter.js" with content "console.log('run output')"

elements.name: unknown command shows command not found error

steps.name: the terminal should contain "command not found"

elements.name: which node reports shell built-in
which nonexistent shows not found in the terminal
grep finds matching lines in a file
find locates files by name pattern
mv renames a file
npm run executes a package.json script

steps.name: I create file "/app/package.json" with content "{\"scripts\":{\"greet\":\"echo npm-script-ran\"}}"
I run terminal command "npm run greet"

elements.name: Editor tab is active by default
Clicking Preview tab shows preview panel and hides editor panel
Clicking Editor tab after Preview restores editor panel
New file button opens a dialog and editor shows the new file

steps.name: I accept the new file dialog with path "/app/newtest.js"

elements.name: Run button executes editor code and shows output in the terminal
File explorer sidebar shows directory entries
clear command exits with code -1

steps.name: the terminal exit code should be -1

elements.name: Ctrl+C cancels typed input and terminal recovers
